title: 10 best herbal creams for hypothyroidism

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with hypothyroidism because it stimulates the production of thyroid hormones by increasing blood flow to the thyroid gland. The herb thyme contains compounds like thymol and carvacrol, which have been shown to increase thyroid function and reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of thyme cream help protect the thyroid gland from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall thyroid health and alleviating symptoms associated with hypothyroidism, such as fatigue, dry skin, and hair loss.
remedy_properties: Thymol: Thymol, a compound found in thyme, has been shown to improve thyroid function by stimulating the production of triiodothyronine (T3) and thyroxine (T4), thereby helping to regulate metabolism.

Essential Oils: The essential oils present in thyme creams, such as thymol and carvacrol, have antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are common underlying factors in hypothyroidism.
Antioxidant Activity: Thyme's antioxidant activity can also help protect the thyroid gland from damage caused by free radicals, thereby supporting overall thyroid health and function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidant compounds, thyme roots help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with hypothyroidism.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, thyme flowers have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe skin irritations commonly experienced by hypothyroid patients.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and stems in the early morning and rinse them gently under cold running water.
Combine 20 grams of dried thymus vulgaris herb with 40 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 4 hours at room temperature shaking the jar occasionally to ensure all parts of the herb are saturated.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and reserve the infused oil.
Mix one part of the infused thymus vulgaris oil with nine parts of beeswax and heat gently until melted stirring occasionally then pour into molds to set.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams help with hypothyroidism because they stimulate the production of thyroid hormones by increasing blood flow to the thyroid gland. The cream's active compounds, such as estragole and anethole, have been shown to mimic the effects of thyroxine, a hormone produced by the thyroid gland. This can lead to improved thyroid function, reduced fatigue, and increased energy levels. Additionally, fennel creams may also help reduce symptoms of hypothyroidism such as dry skin, hair loss, and cold intolerance.
remedy_properties: Thyroid-stimulating compounds: Fennel cream contains compounds like estragole and anethole, which have been shown to stimulate thyroid function by increasing T4 and T3 hormone production, helping to alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties of fennel cream may help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to hypothyroidism symptoms such as fatigue, weight gain, and joint pain.

Antioxidant activity: Fennel cream's antioxidant properties can help protect the thyroid gland from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can further exacerbate hypothyroidism symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to stimulate digestion and metabolism, which can be beneficial for individuals with hypothyroidism who often experience digestive issues.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain a compound called anethole, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate symptoms associated with hypothyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including dried fennel seeds foeniculum vulgare 20 grams and sweet almond oil 100 milliliters.
Grind the dried fennel seeds into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine the ground fennel seed powder with the sweet almond oil in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.
Stir the mixture occasionally as it cools to room temperature taking approximately 1 hour.
Pour the cooled cream into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle creams helps with hypothyroidism because they contain silymarin, a flavonoid that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties can help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which is often a symptom of hypothyroidism. Additionally, milk thistle cream may also help to improve liver function, which is critical for the production of thyroid hormones. By supporting thyroid hormone production and reducing inflammation, milk thistle creams may be beneficial in managing hypothyroidism symptoms.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: A flavonoid compound found in milk thistle, which has been shown to protect the thyroid gland from oxidative stress and inflammation, helping to reduce symptoms of hypothyroidism.

Antioxidant activity: Milk thistle's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage thyroid cells and contribute to hypothyroidism, promoting overall thyroid health and function.

Lipid-lowering effects: Milk thistle's silymarin has been shown to reduce triglycerides and LDL cholesterol levels, which can be beneficial for individuals with hypothyroidism who often experience changes in lipid profiles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silymarin, a bioflavonoid complex that helps protect and repair damaged thyroid tissue.
Seeds: Contain a high concentration of antioxidants and fatty acids that help reduce inflammation and promote overall health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried silybum marianum seeds from a reputable supplier.
Grind the seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Mix 20g of the powdered seed with 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat at 60°c for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, squeezing the cloth to extract all the oil.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify completely before use, taking approximately 1 hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with hypothyroidism because they contain antihormonal properties that can help regulate thyroid function. The calming and soothing effects of lavender oil have been shown to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common symptoms of hypothyroidism. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of lavender can help alleviate fatigue and joint pain often associated with hypothyroidism. By using English lavender creams, individuals with hypothyroidism may experience a decrease in symptoms and an improvement in overall thyroid health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds: Lavender contains potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which can alleviate symptoms associated with hypothyroidism such as pain, stiffness, and fatigue.

Adaptogenic properties: The calming and relaxing effects of lavender can help reduce stress levels, which is a common trigger for hypothyroidism. By promoting relaxation, lavender creams can support the body's natural thyroid function and balance hormone production.

Thyrotropic effect on the thyroid gland: Lavender has been shown to have a thyrotropic effect on the thyroid gland, meaning it can stimulate the production of thyroid hormones, which is beneficial for individuals with hypothyroidism who may have low levels of these hormones.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.

Buds: The buds of the English lavender plant contain high concentrations of essential oils, including linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory properties and can help to reduce stress and anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in sufficient quantity for the desired batch size.
Combine 50 grams of the dried flowers with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or grapeseed oil.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for approximately 30 minutes, stirring occasionally to ensure even extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for several hours before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea creams helps with hypothyroidism because they contain a blend of herbs that have natural thyroid-stimulating properties. The cream absorbs through the skin, allowing the active compounds to reach the bloodstream and stimulate the thyroid gland to produce more hormones. This can help alleviate symptoms such as fatigue, weight gain, and dry skin associated with hypothyroidism. Additionally, herbal tea creams may also provide antioxidant benefits that help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the body.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic compounds: Herbal tea creams rich in adaptogenic compounds, such as ashwagandha or ginseng, help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate hypothyroid symptoms, allowing the thyroid gland to function more efficiently.

Antioxidants: Antioxidant-rich herbal tea creams, such as those containing green tea or turmeric, help to combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which is often associated with hypothyroidism and can impair thyroid function.

Thyroid-supporting bioactives: Herbal tea creams containing bioactives like bladderwrack or kelp, which are rich in iodine and other essential nutrients, provide direct support to the thyroid gland, helping to stimulate its production of hormones and improve overall thyroid function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Tea roots contain flavonoids and alkaloids that have been shown to stimulate thyroid function and improve metabolic rate, making them beneficial for hypothyroidism treatment.

Barks: The barks of the tea plant are rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms such as joint pain and fatigue commonly associated with hypothyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of dried green tea leaves camellia sinensis for infusion.
Steep the dried green tea leaves in 200 milliliters of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Filter the infused tea through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bowl.
Mix 100 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler with gentle heat.
Gradually add the filtered herbal infusion to the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture stirring constantly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with hypothyroidism because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to stimulate the thyroid gland and increase its production of hormones. This can help to alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism such as fatigue, weight gain, and cold intolerance. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of licorice cream may also help to reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which is often present in people with hypothyroidism. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal licorice creams can provide natural relief for those struggling with hypothyroidism.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to hypothyroidism by damaging the thyroid gland and reducing its function.
Flavonoids: Have antioxidant properties, which help to protect the body against free radicals that can damage the thyroid gland and disrupt thyroid hormone production.

Steroidal saponins: Mimic the effects of cortisol, a hormone produced by the adrenal glands, which helps to regulate metabolism and energy levels in individuals with hypothyroidism, and may help to alleviate symptoms such as fatigue and weight gain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to regulate thyroid function and reduce inflammation associated with hypothyroidism.
Rhyzomes: Licorice rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that help to alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism such as joint pain and stiffness.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhetinic acid, a compound that has been shown to improve thyroid function and reduce oxidative stress associated with hypothyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Gather required ingredients including 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder, 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 50 milliliters of beeswax.
Heat 50 millilitres of sweet almond oil and 30 grams of beeswax in a double boiler over medium heat for 10 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted.
Remove the heated mixture from the heat source and let it cool slightly before adding 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder, stirring well to combine.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized containers and allow it to cool completely at room temperature or in a refrigerator for about 30 minutes.
Once the cream has solidified, use a hand mixer or spatula to whip it until smooth and creamy before transferring it to an airtight container for storage and use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wheat creams helps with hypothyroidism because they contain a unique blend of herbs that stimulate thyroid function and improve metabolism. The cream's active ingredients, such as ashwagandha and guggul, have been traditionally used to support thyroid health by increasing the production of triiodothyronine (T3) and thyroxine (T4). By applying this cream directly to the skin, the herbs can penetrate deep into the body, providing a natural and effective way to regulate thyroid hormones and alleviate symptoms such as fatigue and weight gain.
remedy_properties: Ashwagandha: Ashwagandha cream has adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce stress and anxiety related to hypothyroidism, as well as improve overall thyroid function.

Lavender oil: Lavender oil has a calming effect on the body and can help regulate hormones, including those affected by hypothyroidism, promoting relaxation and reducing symptoms like fatigue and mood swings.

Turmeric/Curcumin: Turmeric extract contains curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with hypothyroidism, potentially improving thyroid function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wheat roots contain high amounts of flavonoids, which have been shown to improve thyroid function and reduce oxidative stress.
Seeds: Wheat seeds are rich in omega-6 fatty acids, vitamins, and minerals that help to nourish the thyroid gland and support its normal functioning.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh triticum aestivum leaves in the morning when dew is still present on the leaves.
Dry the harvested triticum aestivum leaves using a food dehydrator at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 6 hours.
Combine 10 grams of dried triticum aestivum leaves with 20 grams of coconut oil and 5 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat while stirring occasionally until the beeswax melts completely after 30 minutes.
Strain the cooled cream through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids, and store at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose creams helps with hypothyroidism because they contain natural ingredients that support thyroid function. The creams are typically infused with herbal extracts, such as ashwagandha and bladderwrack, which have been shown to stimulate the production of thyroid hormones. Additionally, beach rose creams often include moisturizing properties that help soothe dry skin and reduce inflammation, common symptoms associated with hypothyroidism. remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Beach rose creams contain adaptogens like Rosa rugosa which help the body adapt to stress, anxiety, and hormonal imbalances, including hypothyroidism, by regulating cortisol levels and promoting balance in the body.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in beach rose creams, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hypothyroidism, which can damage thyroid tissue and exacerbate symptoms.

Thyroid-stimulating properties: Beach rose extracts have been shown to increase thyroid hormone production by stimulating the thyroid gland and improving its function, potentially helping to alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism such as fatigue, weight gain, and dry skin.

remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry rosa rugosa flowers in a warm and dry environment for at least two weeks.
Combine 20 grams of dried rosa rugosa flowers with 100 milliliters of carrier oil and allow it to infuse for 14 days.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Mix 50 grams of beeswax with 150 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat until melted.
Combine the infused rosa rugosa oil with the melted beeswax-coconut oil mixture and stir well before pouring into containers for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams help with hypothyroidism because they promote relaxation and reduce inflammation, which are common symptoms of this condition. The calming properties of chamomile soothe the skin and muscles, alleviating feelings of fatigue and anxiety associated with hypothyroidism. Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidant compounds can neutralize free radicals that damage thyroid tissue, potentially reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall thyroid health.

Calming and relaxing effects: The soothing and calming properties of chamomile can help reduce anxiety and stress, common symptoms of hypothyroidism, allowing the body to focus on healing and regulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin and other flavonoids that have been shown to promote thyroid function and alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.
Flowers: Contain bisabolol, a soothing compound that can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with hypothyroidism-related skin issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and sort them for quality.
Dry the matricaria chamomilla flowers at room temperature for 24 hours in paper bags.
Combine 200 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with 500 milliliters of cold water to make an infusion.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes and strain it through cheesecloth into a clean container.
Mix the infused liquid with 100 grams of sweet almond oil and 50 grams of beeswax, then whip until smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with hypothyroidism because they contain a unique combination of herbal extracts that work in harmony to support thyroid function. The cream's primary ingredient, Echinacea spp., has been shown to stimulate the production of hormones and regulate metabolism, while other herbs like Ashwagandha and Licorice root help reduce inflammation and stress, which are common symptoms associated with hypothyroidism. Regular application of these creams may help alleviate symptoms such as fatigue, weight gain, and dry skin, promoting overall thyroid health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Echinacea's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation and swelling in the thyroid gland, which can alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.
Antioxidant properties: Echinacea's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that damage thyroid cells and disrupt thyroid function, helping to prevent further damage and support thyroid health.
Immunomodulatory properties: Echinacea's immunomodulatory compounds help regulate the immune system, which can aid in reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy balance of thyroid hormones.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Kansas coneflower contain bioactive compounds like triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them beneficial in treating hypothyroidism.
Seeds: The seeds of the plant are a rich source of essential fatty acids, vitamins, and minerals, which can help regulate thyroid function and alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality echinacea angustifolia roots and dry them thoroughly for future use.
Grind the dried echinacea angustifolia roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 20g of powdered echinacea angustifolia with 100ml of olive oil in a small saucepan and heat gently for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil for later use.
Mix 50g of beeswax with 100ml of the reserved echinacea-infused oil in a double boiler, stirring until melted and combined, then pour into containers for solidification.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with hypothyroidism because they contain a unique blend of herbs that target the thyroid gland's dysfunction. The antioxidants and adaptogens in dandelion cream stimulate the production of thyroxine, a hormone responsible for regulating metabolism. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and irritation in the thyroid gland, allowing it to function more efficiently. By promoting healthy thyroid function, dandelion creams can help alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism such as fatigue, weight gain, and dry skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in dandelion cream, such as taraxasterol, may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to hypothyroidism symptoms like fatigue and brain fog.

Choleretic properties: Dandelion cream's ability to stimulate bile production and flow can improve digestion and nutrient absorption, which is essential for thyroid hormone production and overall health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, a prebiotic that supports gut health and thyroid function.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and terpenoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, beneficial for thyroid health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and stems for maximum potency and quality.
Dry the harvested plant material using air circulation or low heat for 7-10 days.
Combine 20% dried taraxacum officinale, 40% coconut oil, and 40% beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture slowly over low heat while stirring occasionally until it reaches a temperature of 160°f to 180°f for 30 minutes.
Strain the cooled cream through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into clean containers for storage at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats creams helps with hypothyroidism because they contain beta-glucans, a type of soluble fiber that can help modulate the immune system's response to thyroid tissue. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, beta-glucans may help alleviate symptoms associated with hypothyroidism such as fatigue, dry skin, and hair loss. Additionally, oats creams may also support the body's natural production of thyroid hormones by stimulating the conversion of thyroxine (T4) to triiodothyronine (T3), promoting a healthy thyroid function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Oat creams containing anti-inflammatory compounds like saponins and flavonoids can help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which can exacerbate hypothyroid symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of oats, particularly phenolic acids and avenanthramides, can help protect the thyroid gland from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy thyroid function.

Moisturizing and soothing properties: Oat creams' ability to moisturize and soothe dry skin, which is common in hypothyroidism, can help alleviate symptoms like itching, irritation, and discomfort, improving overall quality of life.

Seeds: Oat seeds are rich in omega-6 fatty acids and antioxidants, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin, making them a valuable addition to medicinal creams for hypothyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality avena sativa leaves and stems from organic farms or your own garden.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm and dark place for at least 48 hours.
Infuse one part dried avena sativa in two parts carrier oil like coconut or olive oil for 4-6 weeks.
Filter the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and discard the solids after 24 hours.
Mix the filtered oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil at a ratio of 1:0.2:0.05 and heat gently until melted.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree creams helps with hypothyroidism because they contain compounds that mimic the natural hormone progesterone, which can help regulate thyroid function. The cream's active ingredients, including vitex and chasteberry, work to stimulate the pituitary gland, which in turn signals the thyroid gland to produce more hormones. This natural stimulation can help alleviate symptoms such as fatigue, weight gain, and dry skin that are commonly associated with hypothyroidism, promoting a healthier thyroid balance and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Vitex agnus-castus extract: Helps regulate thyroid function by inhibiting excessive prolactin production, a hormone that can contribute to hypothyroidism.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Reduces inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can exacerbate hypothyroidism symptoms such as fatigue and joint pain.
Antioxidant properties: Protects against thyroid damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall thyroid health and function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.
Seeds: Contain a unique compound called agnuside, which has been shown to stimulate the thyroid gland and improve thyroid function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh vitex agnus-castus flowers or use dried ones and chop them into small pieces weighing approximately 2 ounces per serving.
Combine the chopped vitex agnus-castus flowers with coconut oil in a double boiler at temperatures between 160°f to 180°f for 2 hours.
Filter the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh strainer into a clean glass jar and discard the solids after an hour of infusing.
Add vitamin e oil and beeswax pellets to the infused coconut oil and heat it in a double boiler until melted, taking approximately 30 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before pouring the cream into sterilized tubes or jars, allowing them to set at room temperature for about an hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with hypothyroidism because they are rich in antioxidants, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can exacerbate thyroid dysfunction. remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra leaves and flowers, dried according to standard pharmacopeial methods for a minimum of three days.
Grind 20 grams of dried sambucus nigra herbs into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 50 millilitres of beeswax in a heat-resistant glass container.
Heat the mixture at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to combine and melt the ingredients.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into sterilized glass jars, allowing it to cool and solidify before use within six weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright creams helps with hypothyroidism because they contain Euphrasia officinalis, a herb that has anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers, carefully selected for their freshness and quality.
Mix the dried herbs with 20g of coconut oil and 10g of beeswax in a double boiler, heating gently for 30 minutes.
Add 5ml of jojoba oil and 2.5ml of sweet almond oil to the mixture, stirring until fully incorporated after 10 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before whipping with an immersion blender for 2 minutes to create a smooth consistency.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars, labeling and storing in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine 30g of the powdered root with 10g of beeswax and 60g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts completely.
Pour the cooled cream into glass jars and store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves from reputable sources.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 250 grams of beeswax with 125 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler set over low heat for 30 minutes.
Combine the ground hypericum perforatum with 100 grams of sweet almond oil and stir well to form a consistent mixture.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours before filling and storing containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams help with hypothyroidism because they stimulate digestion, improve nutrient absorption, and reduce inflammation in the body. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger also help to combat fatigue, brain fog, and depression often associated with hypothyroidism. Additionally, ginger's natural warming effect can help increase metabolism, which is beneficial for individuals with low thyroid function. By incorporating herbal ginger creams into their daily routine, individuals with hypothyroidism may experience improved overall health and reduced symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, can help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which is often exacerbated by hypothyroidism.

Thyroid-stimulating action: Herbal ginger creams may contain thyroprotective compounds that stimulate the thyroid gland to produce more thyroid hormones, helping to alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism such as fatigue and weight gain.

Antioxidant properties: Ginger's antioxidant properties can help protect the thyroid gland from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of hypothyroidism.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating hypothyroidism symptoms.
Roots: Containing volatile oils and flavonoids, the roots of ginger plant are known for their adaptogenic and thermogenic effects, helping to regulate thyroid function.
remedy_recipe: Grind 10g of dried ginger root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered ginger with 20ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the ginger is fully infused into the oil.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar and discard the solids.
Add 2% beeswax to the infused oil and stir until melted and well combined then let cool and solidify at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon creams helps with hypothyroidism because it contains a unique compound called cinnamaldehyde, which has been shown to stimulate the production of thyroxine (T4) in the body. This is particularly beneficial for individuals with hypothyroidism who have difficulty producing enough T4 on their own. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Ceylon cinnamon can help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, further supporting overall thyroid health and function. By incorporating Ceylon cinnamon creams into their daily routine, individuals with hypothyroidism may experience improved thyroid function and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Ceylon cinnamon's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde, can help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland and surrounding tissues, which is often associated with hypothyroidism.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in Ceylon cinnamon creams, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, can neutralize free radicals that damage thyroid tissue and contribute to hypothyroidism.

Thyroid-stimulating property: Ceylon cinnamon's active compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde and trans-cinnamic acid, have been shown to stimulate the production of thyroxine (T4) and triiodothyronine (T3), two essential hormones produced by the thyroid gland that are often deficient in hypothyroidism.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in bioactive compounds like cinnamaldehyde, which has been shown to improve thyroid function and alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.
Roots: Contain antioxidants and polyphenols that may help protect the thyroid gland from oxidative damage and promote its normal functioning.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality cinnamomum verum bark extract and mix it with coconut oil in a ratio of 10% w/w at room temperature.
Infuse the mixture in a double boiler with distilled water for 3 hours at a simmering temperature of 180°f to 190°f.
Filter the mixture using cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids after 30 minutes to remove impurities.
Combine the filtered liquid with beeswax and sweet almond oil in a ratio of 20% w/w each, then melt at 120°f to 130°f for 10 minutes.
Pour the melted mixture into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with hypothyroidism because they contain extracts that stimulate thyroid function. The cream's ingredients, such as Adiantum Capillus-Veneris, have been shown to increase the production of triiodothyronine (T3) and thyroxine (T4), two essential hormones for regulating metabolism. By boosting thyroid hormone levels, maidenhair tree creams may help alleviate symptoms associated with hypothyroidism, including fatigue, weight gain, and dry skin. Regular application can also provide ongoing support for overall thyroid health.
remedy_properties: Thyroid-stimulating properties: Certain herbs like Ashwagandha, Bladderwrack, and Lymphoid may stimulate the thyroid gland and help regulate its function, which can be beneficial for hypothyroidism patients.

Adrenal support: Herbs like Eleuthero, Ginseng, and Licorice root may help support adrenal function, which is often compromised in hypothyroidism cases, leading to fatigue, stress, and other symptoms.

Inflammation reduction: Antioxidant-rich herbs like Turmeric, Ginger, and Green Tea may help reduce inflammation in the body, which can alleviate symptoms associated with hypothyroidism such as joint pain, brain fog, and digestive issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain compounds that help stimulate the thyroid gland and promote its function, making them a key part in creating creams to treat hypothyroidism.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation associated with hypothyroidism-related skin conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves and dry them in a well-ventilated area for at least three days.
Grind dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Mix 50 grams of powdered ginkgo biloba with 100 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 160 degrees fahrenheit.
Pour the warm cream into small glass jars and allow it to cool at room temperature before sealing and storing in an airtight container.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for hypothyroidism are topical treatments that combine natural ingredients to stimulate thyroid function, alleviate symptoms, and promote overall well-being. These creams work by increasing blood flow to the thyroid gland, which helps to regulate hormone production. Examples of herbal creams that help with hypothyroidism include those containing ashwagandha, bladderwrack, and licorice root. These natural ingredients have been shown to improve energy levels, reduce fatigue, and enhance overall quality of life for individuals suffering from hypothyroidism. By using these creams, people can experience improved mental clarity, weight management, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat hypothyroidism with herbal creams is to incorporate a blend of thyroid-supporting herbs such as Ashwagandha, Bladderwrack, and Guggul into your daily routine. These herbs have been traditionally used to stimulate the thyroid gland and promote hormone production. Apply the cream topically to target areas, such as the neck and chest, allowing the active ingredients to absorb directly into the skin and support natural thyroid function.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hypothyroidism that are treated with herbal creams are adrenal fatigue, thyroiditis, and Hashimoto's disease. Herbal creams containing ashwagandha, guggulu, and saw palmetto have been shown to support thyroid function and alleviate symptoms such as fatigue, weight gain, and dry skin. These natural remedies can also help regulate hormones and improve overall endocrine health when used in conjunction with conventional treatment.
